item.title: Amazon Kicks Off Debut Swiss Franc Bond Sale in Record Six Parts
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. has begun the sale of its first Swiss franc bonds as it looks to raise a record six-part deal in the currency.

item.title: Ethiopian in Talks With Airbus for Widebody, Regional Jet Order
item.description: Ethiopian Airlines Group is in early talks with Airbus SE to purchase regional and widebody aircraft as Africa’s largest carrier looks to expand its foothold on the continent and beyond.

item.title: Bain, LY Said to Offer to Buy Kakaku, Rivaling EQT Takeover Bid
item.description: Bain Capital and LY Corp. have submitted a joint offer to acquire Tokyo-listed Kakaku.com Inc., the $3.7 billion price comparison site operator fielding a takeover by EQT AB.

item.title: Bristol-Myers Inks Up to $15 Billion Licensing Deal With Hengrui
item.description: Bristol-Myers Squibb Co. inked a collaboration and licensing deal with Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals Co. that could be valued at as much as $15.2 billion, as the US drugmaker seeks to leverage China’s efficiency in early development.

item.title: Hitachi Said to Mull $640 Million Stake Sale in Machine Firm
item.description: Japanese industrial electronics maker Hitachi Ltd. is looking to offload about 8% of Hitachi Construction Machinery Co. shares in block trades, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Vodafone Sales Growth Beats as German Service Revenue Rises
item.description: Vodafone Group Plc reported organic revenue growth last quarter that beat analysts’ expectations as the mobile operator focuses on its biggest markets and Germany returned to growth.

item.title: Prosus Targets $3.6 Billion From Just Eat Sales in Europe Push
item.description: Prosus NV is targeting $3.6 billion in revenue from Just Eat Takeaway.com in a year as it looks to boost efficiency in its European food-delivery business at a time of rapid consolidation in the sector.

item.title: Paschi Beats on Profit as CEO Focuses on Mediobanca Integration
item.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA’s profit in the first quarter beat expectations, giving tailwind to Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio and his effort to integrate Mediobanca SpA.

item.title: UK Bonds and Pound Slump as Pressure on Starmer Builds
Motsepe Says Partnerships Boosting South Africa’s Mining Sector
item.description: South Africa has regained competitiveness in mining because of partnerships between the public and private sector to tackle regulatory issues and structural bottlenecks, according to billionaire Patrice Motsepe.

item.title: Uniper’s Trading Arm Returns to Profit as Gas Business Improves
item.description: Uniper SE’s trading division was back in profit in the first quarter as its gas division performed better and wasn’t impacted by supply disruptions related to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp Trims Sales View While Keeping Profit Target Intact
item.description: Thyssenkrupp AG maintained its key earnings and cash-flow targets, while striking a more cautious note on sales because of heightened geopolitical uncertainty and its impact on international markets.

item.title: Taiwan Brokerages Seek About $1 Billion of Loans as Market Booms
item.description: Two of Taiwan’s biggest securities firms are seeking nearly $1 billion of loans to expand their businesses, according to people familiar with the matter, as investors pile into a booming stock market that has become the sixth largest in the world.

item.title: Jollibee Shares Drop to Five-Year Low as Profit Sinks 39% on CPI
item.description: Jollibee Foods Corp.’s profit fell 39% in the first quarter as costs surged, prompting the Philippines’ largest fastfood chain operator to review its targets and spending plan. Shares fell to the lowest in five years.

item.title: China LNG Imports Hint at Recovery as Buyers Replace Lost Supply
item.description: Liquefied natural gas deliveries to China are showing signs of recovery, as some buyers replace shipments disrupted by the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Malaysia Mulls Further Fuel Subsidy Cuts Even as Election Looms
item.description: Malaysia will continue with its fuel subsidy reform despite the possibility of a general election this year, according to Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong.

item.title: Chinese Stocks See Risk Appetite Revival as Margin Loans Jump
item.description: Risk appetite is returning to China’s tech sector, with margin lending, trading volumes and benchmarks all climbing back to highs thanks to growing bets that a recent rally will continue.

item.title: The EU’s Commission Chief Is Increasingly Seen as Too Powerful
item.description: Ursula von der Leyen is facing pushback from some member countries.

item.title: Europe Exports Spyware to Human Rights Abusers, Watchdog Says
item.description: European companies have sold controversial surveillance technologies to countries known for violating human rights, a new report has found.

item.title: Wheat Extends Gains as Poor Weather Worsens US Crop Conditions
item.description: Chicago wheat extended gains as crop conditions in the US worsened due to persistent dryness in some key growing areas.

item.title: Copper Steadies Near Record as Trump Rejects Iran’s Peace Plan
item.description: Copper steadied near its highest close on record after US President Donald Trump dismissed Iran’s proposals for a peace deal and said the ceasefire with Tehran was on “life support”.

item.title: Japan’s 10-Year Bond Sale Sees Firm Demand on Higher Yields
item.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond auction Tuesday saw stronger demand than the 12-month average as higher yields underpinned buying.

item.title: Quadria-Backed Nobel Hygiene Said to Plan $300 Million India IPO
item.description: Nobel Hygiene Pvt., a maker of diapers and similar disposable products, is considering an initial public offering in India that could raise as much as $300 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Australia Watchdog Says Money Launderers Ramping Up AI for Scams
item.description: Australia’s financial crimes watchdog warned of a heightened threat of money laundering linked to artificial intelligence that has been used by crooks to scale up activities, automate processes and create fake documents.

item.title: Trump Delays Move to Lower Tariffs on Beef Imports, WSJ Says
item.description: The Trump administration is delaying a plan to suspend tariffs on imported beef, according to the Wall Street Journal.

item.title: Bond Selloff Points to Rate-Hike Jolt for Indian Equities Ahead
item.description: Modi’s call to curb fuel use suggests an imminent hike in pump prices, adding to inflationary pressure.

item.title: JX Metals Shares Drop Most Since April 2025 on Convertible Bond
item.description: Shares of Japanese metals refiner JX Advanced Metals Corp. slid after the company unveiled a plan to issue ¥250 billion ($1.6 billion) of bonds that can be converted into stocks to fund share buybacks.

item.title: Korea Roils Markets by Floating ‘Citizen Dividend’ From AI Tax
item.description: A top South Korean policymaker said the nation should pay citizens a “dividend” using taxes on AI profits, underscoring growing pressure on authorities to redistribute gains from a boom that has enriched chipmakers like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.

item.title: Franklin Templeton Turns to Fixed Income to Fuel India Growth
item.description: Franklin Templeton is betting that Indian investors’ rising interest in fixed income will power the next leg of its growth in the country, where it is regaining ground after a credit crunch six years ago.

item.title: DroneShield Plunges as Regulator Probes Filings, Share Trades
item.description: DroneShield Ltd.’s stock tumbled to the lowest in about three months after the Australian counter-drone firm said Australia’s financial regulator was reviewing disclosures and share trades from November.
